assumptions: Nutrition values are practical estimates based on the stated family portion sizes.
The fixed external school snack contributes estimated carbs, fat and fiber to daily totals because only its calories and protein are specified.
Cooked weights are used for grains, potatoes, pasta, meat and fish in recipes; the shopping list gives dry equivalents where useful for buying.
Child calcium support is structured through low-fat dairy, fortified soy foods, kefir, yogurt, skyr, milk and sardines with bones.

recipes.title: Oat, skyr, berry, flax and walnut breakfast bowls
instructions: Simmer oats with water and cinnamon until creamy.
Divide into three bowls and top with skyr, berries, flaxseed and walnuts.
Serve the child portion with kefir for extra calcium.

recipes.title: Soy milk oat porridge with skyr, banana, chia and walnuts
instructions: Simmer oats with fortified soy milk and cinnamon, stirring often.
Top each bowl with skyr, banana, chia seeds and walnuts.
Give the husband the largest oat portion; keep the wife portion walnut-forward but calorie controlled.

recipes.title: Barley-oat skyr bowls with kiwi, berries and walnuts
instructions: Warm cooked barley with oats and water until thick.
Top with skyr, berries, kiwi, flaxseed and walnuts.
Serve the child portion with milk.

recipes.title: Overnight soy oats with skyr, berries, flax and walnuts
instructions: Mix oats, fortified soy milk, skyr, flaxseed and cinnamon in a covered bowl.
Refrigerate overnight.
Top with berries and walnuts before serving.

recipes.title: Cottage cheese oat pancakes with berry yogurt and walnuts
instructions: Stir oats, cottage cheese, eggs, egg whites, baking powder and cinnamon into a thick batter.
Cook small pancakes in a lightly oiled pan.
Serve with Greek yogurt, berries and walnuts; the wife portion uses a smaller pancake stack.

recipes.title: Buckwheat-oat skyr bowls with berries, chia and walnuts
instructions: Warm cooked buckwheat with oats and water until soft and thick.
Top with skyr, berries, chia and walnuts.
Serve the child portion with kefir.

recipes.title: Barley-oat skyr bowls with mango, chia and walnuts
instructions: Warm barley and oats with fortified soy milk and water.
Top with skyr, mango, chia and walnuts.
Use a larger grain portion for the husband and child.

recipes.title: Turkey barley vegetable bowls with pumpkin seeds
instructions: Warm turkey, barley, broccoli and carrots together on the stovetop.
Fold in bell peppers and leafy greens.
Dress with olive oil, lemon juice, pumpkin seeds and parsley.

recipes.title: Chicken, red lentil and vegetable stew with brown rice
instructions: Cook onion, carrot and garlic in olive oil until softened.
Add tomatoes, lentils, stock, chicken and cumin; simmer until thick.
Stir in spinach and serve with brown rice. Pack the extra portions for Tuesday lunch.

recipes.title: Oven salmon with potatoes, broccoli and tomato-cucumber salad
instructions: Warm salmon and potatoes in the oven while steaming broccoli.
Toss cucumber and tomatoes with lemon juice, olive oil and dill.
Serve the husband the largest salmon portion and pack extra salmon, potatoes and vegetables for Wednesday lunch.

recipes.title: Tofu, chickpea and vegetable stir-fry with brown rice
instructions: Brown tofu cubes in olive oil, then add garlic and ginger.
Add broccoli, bell peppers, carrots, cabbage and chickpeas; cook until crisp-tender.
Finish with lime juice and sesame seeds, then serve over brown rice. Pack the extra portions for Thursday lunch.

recipes.title: Lean beef, kidney bean and barley chili
instructions: Cook onion, carrots and bell peppers in olive oil until softened.
Add beef, beans, barley, tomatoes, corn, paprika and cumin; simmer until thick.
Stir in leafy greens near the end. Pack the extra portions for Friday lunch.

recipes.title: Cod, pea and potato soup with spinach
instructions: Cook leek and carrots in olive oil, then add potatoes, peas and enough water to cover.
Blend part of the soup with an immersion blender for body.
Add milk, cod, spinach, dill and black pepper; heat gently without boiling hard.

recipes.title: Chicken quinoa salad with edamame, avocado and citrus
instructions: Combine quinoa, edamame, leafy greens, cucumber, tomatoes and citrus.
Top with chicken, avocado and sunflower seeds.
Dress with olive oil and lemon juice.

recipes.title: Oven mackerel with barley pilaf and roasted vegetables
instructions: Roast mackerel, beetroot, carrots and broccoli until the fish flakes and vegetables are tender.
Warm barley and fold through shredded cabbage.
Mix yogurt, dill and lemon juice for a light sauce.

recipes.title: Sardine, white bean and potato salad with greens
instructions: Toss potatoes, white beans, cucumber, tomatoes and leafy greens.
Top with sardines and parsley.
Dress with olive oil and lemon juice; use no added salt.

recipes.title: Chicken whole-grain pasta with spinach, tomato and zucchini
instructions: Cook zucchini, mushrooms and garlic in olive oil until softened.
Add tomatoes, chicken, spinach and cooked pasta; warm through.
Stir in yogurt off the heat and finish with basil.

recipes.title: Kefir-skyr berry chia cups
instructions: Stir kefir and skyr until smooth.
Top with berries and chia seeds.

recipes.title: Greek yogurt berry walnut cups
instructions: Divide yogurt into bowls.
Top with berries, walnuts and flaxseed.

recipes.title: Skyr melon pumpkin seed bowls
instructions: Divide skyr into bowls.
Top with melon and pumpkin seeds.

recipes.title: Kefir banana chia drink
instructions: Blend kefir and banana with an immersion blender.
Stir in chia seeds and let stand for a few minutes.

recipes.title: Fortified soy yogurt berry walnut cups
instructions: Divide fortified soy yogurt into bowls.
Top with berries, walnuts and chia seeds.

recipes.title: Kefir cottage cheese cucumber bowls
instructions: Divide cottage cheese into bowls and spoon kefir around it.
Top with cucumber, pumpkin seeds and dill.

recipes.title: Greek yogurt grape sunflower cups
instructions: Divide Greek yogurt into bowls.
Top with grapes, sunflower seeds and chia seeds.

checks.label: Fatty fish ≥2x this week
checks.detail: Tuesday salmon dinner with Wednesday leftover lunch; Saturday mackerel dinner; Sunday sardine lunch.

checks.label: Legumes ≥3x this week
checks.detail: Monday dinner and Tuesday lunch lentils; Wednesday dinner and Thursday lunch chickpeas; Thursday dinner and Friday lunch kidney beans; Friday dinner peas; Sunday white beans.

checks.label: Soy foods 4–7 inclusions
checks.detail: Fortified soy milk Tuesday, Thursday and Sunday breakfasts; tofu Wednesday dinner and Thursday lunch; fortified soy yogurt Friday snack; edamame Saturday lunch.

checks.label: Red meat ≤2x (adults)
checks.detail: One generated red-meat recipe: Thursday dinner with Friday leftover lunch.

checks.label: Wife sat fat ≤11g/day average
checks.detail: Estimated average 9.5 g/day; highest day is 11.0 g.

checks.label: Wife beta-glucan ≥3g/day (oats/barley daily)
checks.detail: Oats or barley included at breakfast every day; additional barley included Monday lunch, Thursday dinner, Friday lunch and Saturday dinner.

checks.label: Child protein ≥70g/day average
checks.detail: Estimated average 101.3 g/day including fixed external school snack on school days.

checks.label: Child calcium structurally ~1300mg/day
checks.detail: Daily calcium sources include skyr, kefir, yogurt, cottage cheese, low-fat milk, fortified soy milk, fortified soy yogurt, tofu and sardines with bones.

checks.label: Protein floor met at breakfast (all persons)
checks.detail: Every breakfast uses skyr, yogurt, cottage cheese, egg whites, soy milk or dairy with oats or barley.

checks.label: Protein floor met at lunch (all persons)
checks.detail: All lunches meet husband ≥35 g, wife ≥28 g and child ≥20 g protein estimates.

checks.label: Shared snack ≥4 days
checks.detail: Shared snack included Monday through Sunday.

checks.label: Weekday active cooking ≤30 min
checks.detail: All weekday generated meals have active time of 25 minutes or less; overnight oats require 10 active minutes.

checks.label: No excluded fruit terms
checks.detail: Generated meals use only allowed produce choices such as berries, banana, kiwi, mango, melon, grapes and citrus.

checks.label: No generated processed meat
checks.detail: Generated meals use poultry, fish, tofu, legumes, eggs, dairy and one lean beef recipe only.

checks.label: Fixed child snack accounted for, not modified
checks.detail: The fixed external snack object is copied unchanged on Monday through Friday and included in child totals.

checks.label: Sodium not stacked on high-risk days
checks.detail: School days avoid salty fish and heavy cheese; canned legumes are rinsed; higher-sodium fish is placed on the weekend.
